error.title: Something went wrong
error.body: This page encountered an error. You can try again or navigate to another section using the sidebar.

dashboard.title: Compliance Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Your compliance readiness across every framework

clauses.title: ISO 27001:2022 Clause Tracker
clauses.subtitle: Track your progress through clauses 4–10. All descriptions below are original paraphrases, not ISO text.
clauses.selectClause: Select a clause to view details

assets.title: Asset Inventory
assets.listDescription: Every asset under management — classified, owned, and linked to its risks.
assets.name: Name
assets.controlsNote: Tag controls related to this asset for traceability.

empty.filtersHint: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
empty.recordsHint: Register the systems, applications, and data stores in scope before mapping risks and controls.

crit.heading: Asset Criticality
crit.confidentialityHelp: Impact of unauthorised disclosure. 1 = public, 5 = highly sensitive / regulated.
crit.integrityHelp: Impact of unauthorised modification or loss of accuracy. 1 = trivial, 5 = safety/financial-critical.
crit.availabilityHelp: Impact of an outage. 1 = tolerable, 5 = business-critical / no downtime acceptable.

modal.newDescription: Register an information asset to track inside risk + control coverage.
modal.discardConfirm: Discard asset? Any details you entered will be lost.
modal.editDiscardConfirm: Discard changes? Any edits you made will be lost.

detail.activityDesc: A dedicated activity feed for this asset is on the roadmap. Tenant-wide audit log is available from Admin → Audit Log.

risks.title: Risk Register
risks.listDescription: The single register of business risk — track, treat, and evidence.

eval.title: Risk Evaluation
eval.likelihoodHelp: Inherent probability of this scenario in the next 12 months, ignoring current controls. 1 = rare, 5 = almost certain.
eval.impactHelp: Severity to the organisation if the risk materialises. 1 = minor / isolated, 5 = catastrophic / regulatory.

history.title: History
history.empty: No history yet. Daily snapshots accrue once the risk-snapshot job has run.

bowtie.title: Bow-Tie Analysis
bowtie.description: Threats (left) → preventive barriers → the risk event → mitigating barriers → consequences (right). Barrier colour = control effectiveness.

fair.title: FAIR Analysis
fair.intro: Calibrate each factor as a range — give the interval you're 90% sure contains the true value. The simulation samples the full range; the derived point is the Beta-PERT mean.

assessment.kriNudge: A leading signal has moved since this risk was last assessed — re-assess the likelihood and impact below to bring the conclusion in line with the indicator.
assessment.noEffectivenessSignal: no effectiveness signal (run a test or declare effectiveness)
assessment.deriveResidualSuffix: to derive a residual from your control stack.
assessment.legacyDimsUnknown: Set before decomposition — dimensions unknown (legacy formula).
assessment.baselineWarning: Inherent has changed since you opened the residual draft — your residual now applies to L={l} × I={i}. Re-check the dimensions before saving.
assessment.justificationPlaceholder: Why this residual differs from the derived suggestion
assessment.quantifiedHelp: This risk already carries a FAIR loss estimate. Review or refine the analysis.
assessment.notQuantifiedHelp: Need loss numbers instead of bands? Run the FAIR analysis on this risk.
assessment.residualNotDerivable: No derivable residual — link controls with an effectiveness signal.

histogramView.title: ALE histogram
histogramView.description: Each quantified risk lands in a log-scale loss bucket, stacked by its matrix band — the distribution the heatmap structurally compresses.
histogramView.empty: No quantified risks yet — set FAIR ranges or SLE × ARO on a risk to populate the histogram.

collisions.title: Cell collisions
collisions.description: Risks sharing a matrix cell whose loss estimates differ more than 10× — the matrix cannot show the difference.

emptyFilter.title: No risks match your filters
emptyFilter.description: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.

new.title: New risk
new.descShort: Capture a risk against your register.
new.descLong: Capture a risk and optionally pre-fill from a template.
new.templateDesc: Pre-fills title, category, likelihood, and impact from the selected template.

detail.aleAria: Annualised loss expectancy {ale} — open the quantification tab

ai.title: AI-Assisted Risk Assessment
ai.subtitle: Generate and review AI-suggested risks for your organization
ai.contextPlaceholder: Describe your industry, business model, regulatory environment, or specific concerns...
ai.analyzingDesc: The AI is generating risk suggestions based on your assets, frameworks, and context.
ai.analyzingHint: This may take a few seconds
ai.creatingDesc: Adding accepted suggestions to your Risk Register.
ai.doneHeading: {count, plural, one {# risk added to your register} other {# risks added to your register}}
ai.doneDesc: AI suggestions have been applied. You can review and refine them in the Risk Register.
ai.selectOne: Select at least one suggestion to accept
ai.assetsNone: No assets found. Suggestions will be general.

aiSystems.title: AI Systems
aiSystems.description: {total, plural, one {# AI system} other {# AI systems}} registered · {high} high-risk · {prohibited} prohibited. Each classified against the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) and mapped to its obligations.
aiSystems.emptyDesc: Register an AI system to classify its EU AI Act risk tier and map it to the obligations that tier requires.

new.title: Register AI system
new.descShort: Register an AI system and classify its EU AI Act risk tier.
new.descLong: Classify against the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689).
new.useContextPlaceholder: Where and how it is deployed…
new.classificationHelp: Answer what applies. Leave everything blank for a minimal-risk system. The tier is computed on the server.
new.art6Checkbox: Article 6(1) — safety component of an Annex I regulated product

detail.conformityHelp: Generate a DRAFT for human review. A draft is queued in the approval queue — nothing is published, and a Declaration of Conformity is never auto-issued.
detail.draftsQueuedSingular: Draft queued for review in the
detail.draftsQueuedPlural: Drafts queued for review in the

monteCarlo.title: Loss exceedance (Monte Carlo)
monteCarlo.empty: No simulation yet — run one to compute the portfolio loss distribution, VaR, and the loss exceedance curve.
monteCarlo.lecDesc: For each annual-loss threshold (x), the simulated probability the year's total losses are ≥ that threshold.
monteCarlo.breachNote: ≈{pct}% chance the year's losses exceed the {money} portfolio appetite.
monteCarlo.perRiskNote: {over} of {total} simulated risks carry a P90 loss above the {money} per-risk appetite cap.

correlations.title: Risk Correlation Matrix
correlations.clickHint: Click a cell in the upper triangle to edit a pair.

hierarchy.title: Risk Hierarchy
hierarchy.empty: No nodes yet. Add a node, then link risks to it (via the hierarchy API or the risk form).

kri.title: Key Risk Indicators
kri.name: Name
kri.empty: No KRIs yet. Create one to track a leading indicator with RAG thresholds.

lossEvents.title: Loss events
lossEvents.intro: The system predicts losses everywhere — FAIR ALE, the simulated portfolio P50/P90, the loss-exceedance curve. This is where the world's answer comes back, so the forecasts can be scored.
lossEvents.emptyActuals: No actuals recorded yet — the forecasting stack is unfalsifiable until losses come back in. Record the next one below, or enable the finding / incident close-out integration to record them automatically.
lossEvents.whatHappenedPlaceholder: Customer breach response — settlement + vendor X

scenarios.title: Risk Scenarios
scenarios.intro: Create a what-if scenario, then simulate to compare its portfolio VaR against the live baseline. Overrides (field patches / synthetic risks) are authored via the scenarios API.
scenarios.name: Name

controls.title: Controls Library
controls.listDescription: Every control mapped to its requirements and evidence.
controls.name: Control Name
controls.assetsNote: Tag assets protected by this control for traceability.
controls.justificationPlaceholder: Explain why this control does not apply to your organization...
controls.justificationRequired: Justification is required when marking a control as Not Applicable

empty.filterDesc: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
empty.recordsDesc: Start with a pre-built framework or define your own control.

new.title: New Control
new.desc: Create a custom control for your register.
new.frequencyHint: How often the control is expected to be reviewed or re-tested (monthly, quarterly, annually). Drives the next-review date on the control dashboard.
new.applicabilityTooltip: Mark Not Applicable when scope legitimately excludes this control (e.g. no payment processing for PCI). The justification is required and appears verbatim in the SoA report.

fields.name: Name
fields.title: Title
fields.ownerHint: Search members to assign, or clear to unassign.

errors.nameMin: Name must be at least 3 characters — not saved.

detailPage.syncConflictContent: Local and remote state diverged — resolve before editing.
detailPage.syncFailedContent: The integration could not reach the source system.
detailPage.couldntLoadEvidenceDesc: Something went wrong fetching this control's evidence. Reload the page to try again.

errors.titleMinLength: Title must be at least 3 characters.
errors.chooseFile: Choose a file to upload, or enter an evidence URL.

detailPage.activityEmptyDesc: Status changes, link updates, and edits show up here once anything moves.

editModal.title: Edit Control
editModal.annualCostHelp: Operate + maintain spend per year. Leave at 0 if not priced — the ROI surface shows an honest gap rather than fabricating a number.

newTask.title: Create Task
newTask.desc: Add a task linked to this control.

roi.headline: Reduces expected loss by {ale} on {cost} spend — {roi}× ROI.
roi.gapSubline: ROI is null on purpose — the model never invents a number from missing inputs.
roi.sublineOne: Across {quantified} of {linked} linked risk with a quantified ALE, priced at {effectiveness}% declared effectiveness.
roi.sublineOther: Across {quantified} of {linked} linked risks with a quantified ALE, priced at {effectiveness}% declared effectiveness.

mappingsTab.errorDesc: Something went wrong fetching this control's framework mappings. Reload the page to try again.
mappingsTab.emptyDesc: Map this control to specific framework requirements to track coverage.

evidenceTab.emptyDesc: Link existing evidence or upload new files to satisfy this control.

bestValue.empty: No control yet carries a price + effectiveness + a quantified linked risk. The leaderboard appears once at least one does.

sankey.title: Controls flow
sankey.subtitle: How assets expose risks and how controls mitigate them, at a glance.

dashboard.title: Controls Dashboard
dashboard.allPassed: All checks passed — no issues found

templates.title: Control Templates
templates.subtitle: Select templates to install as controls in your register
templates.emptyDesc: Templates are seeded by your admin.

evidence.title: Evidence Library
evidence.listDescription: Proof for every control — collected, reviewed, and audit-ready.

text.title: Add evidence
text.description: Record a link, narrative, or attestation against a control.
text.folderPlaceholder: e.g. SOC2/2026 or Quarterly access reviews

list.bulkApproveDesc: Selected evidence will be marked Approved. This bypasses the review step.
list.archivedNoticeBody: Archived evidence should not be used in active audit packs or compliance assessments. Unarchive if you need to reuse this evidence.
list.emptyFilterDesc: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
list.emptyRecordsDesc: Upload screenshots, exports, and attestations that prove your controls work in practice.

edit.title: Edit evidence
edit.description: Update the details of this evidence record.
edit.discardConfirm: Discard changes? Any edits you made will be lost.
edit.retentionDesc: When this evidence's retention period ends. Clear to remove the retention policy.
edit.retentionFailed: Saved details, but failed to update retention

upload.title: Upload evidence
upload.descriptionModal: Drag and drop one or more files — PDF, Office, CSV, image, JSON, or ZIP. Each file becomes its own evidence record.
upload.descriptionHeader: Drag and drop one or more files (or click to browse). Files share the metadata you set below.
upload.dropHint: PDF, Office, CSV, image, JSON, or ZIP — up to {mb} MB per file
upload.titlePlaceholderMulti: Each file uses its own filename
upload.titleMultiHint: Per-file titles default to the filename when uploading multiple files.
upload.retainTooltip: After this date the evidence is archived out of the active set. It stays in the audit log — admins can still recover it.
upload.retainHint: Optional — applies to every file in the batch.
upload.searchControlsDesc: Search across {count, plural, one {# control} other {# controls}} by annex id, code, or name.
upload.folderDesc: Optional — group this upload with related evidence.
upload.fileRequired: A file is required to upload evidence.
upload.controlsUnavailable: No controls available to link yet.

bulkImport.title: Bulk import evidence
bulkImport.descriptionModal: Upload a ZIP of evidence files to extract into individual evidence records.
bulkImport.descriptionHeader: Drop a .zip and the worker will extract each supported file into its own evidence record.
bulkImport.dropHint: Up to 100 MB per archive. PDF / Office / image / CSV / JSON entries are extracted; other entries are skipped.

policies.title: Policy Management
policies.listDescription: Authored, approved, and acknowledged — your live policy library.
policies.selectPolicy: Select a policy to view details

list.emptyFilterDesc: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
list.emptyDesc: Author the documents that govern how your organisation operates — security, privacy, acceptable use, incident response.

new.descBlank: Create a blank policy and add content later.
new.descTemplate: Select a template to start with pre-written content.
new.discard: Discard policy? Any details you entered will be lost.
new.contentPlaceholder:
# Policy Content

Write your policy here in Markdown...

detail.markReviewedTooltip: Records a periodic review of this policy and updates the next-review date. This does not change the publication status — to publish, use Request Approval → Publish in the Versions tab.
detail.guideApprovedBody: Open the Versions tab and click Publish to make this the live policy.
detail.guideDraftBody: Edit the content in the Editor tab, then open the Versions tab and click Request Approval to start the publication workflow (review → approval → publish).
detail.createVersionHint: Create a version in the Editor tab.
detail.editorPlaceholder:
# Policy Content

Write your policy here...
detail.externalUrlHint: Link to an external policy document (Google Docs, Confluence, etc.)

evidence.title: Evidence to Retain
evidence.note: Suggested from the policy template — link each item to the evidence that proves the policy operates.

sharepoint.conflict: The SharePoint version is newer than the last sync — pull first to avoid overwriting it.
sharepoint.linkDesc: Link this policy to a SharePoint document for two-way sync.

templates.emptyDesc: Policy templates will appear here once your tenant or admin loads them.
templates.emptyFilterDesc: Try clearing the category filter or broadening your search.
templates.suggestBody: This template maps to {count, plural, one {# control} other {# controls}} you already have installed. These are suggestions — review before relying on them for an audit. Linking a policy to a control records that the policy contributes to satisfying it.
templates.curatedNote: Curated mappings are our judgment, not an authoritative compliance claim — they start unchecked so you opt in explicitly.
templates.linkNControls: Link {count, plural, one {# control} other {# controls}}

audits.title: Internal Audits
audits.listDescription: Cycles, packs, and findings — the audit trail your assessors will see.
audits.selectAudit: Select an audit to view details

newModal.description: Plan a new internal audit. The default checklist is generated automatically; you can edit it after creation.
newModal.discardConfirm: Discard audit? Any details you entered will be lost.

cycles.title: Audit Readiness
cycles.cycleCount: {count, plural, one {# audit cycle} other {# audit cycles}}
cycles.frameworkHint: The compliance framework this audit cycle tracks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIS2…). Controls and evidence are filtered by this on the cycle dashboard.
cycles.auditPeriodHint: The reporting window the cycle evidences. Pick a preset for the usual quarterly / annual audits, or choose a custom range on the calendar. Optional — you can set this later.
cycles.emptyDesc: Create your first audit cycle for ISO 27001 or NIS2.

cycleDetail.previewError: Could not load default pack preview.
cycleDetail.totalItems: Total: {count} items will be included in the default pack.

packs.celebrateDesc: {name} — frozen and shareable with your auditor.
packs.itemsEmptyTitle: No items in this pack yet
packs.itemsEmptyDesc: Add evidence, controls, or risks to this audit pack from the source pages.

auditor.assignedPacksEmptyDesc: You have not been assigned any audit packs yet.

readinessOverview.title: Audit Readiness
readinessOverview.subtitle: Framework readiness scores across all audit cycles
readinessOverview.cyclesEmptyDesc: Create an audit cycle to see readiness scores.

bia.description: {total, plural, one {# process} other {# processes}} analysed · {critical} critical · {overdue, plural, one {# review} other {# reviews}} overdue. Ordered by recovery priority — what comes back first.
bia.emptyDesc: Analyse a critical process — set its RTO/RPO/MTPD and criticality — to satisfy NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) business continuity.
bia.createDescShort: Analyse a process's recovery objectives for business continuity.
bia.createDescLong: Analyse a process's recovery objectives (ISO 22301 / NIS2 Art.21(2)(c)).
bia.phNotes: Single points of failure, recovery gaps, dependency notes…

biaDetail.notAttached: Not attached to a modeled process node.
biaDetail.controlsLink: {count, plural, one {# continuity control} other {# continuity controls}} link this BIA as evidence.
biaDetail.frameworkDesc: Business continuity and crisis management — this analysis is the operational artifact for the requirement.

nis2Gap.loadFailed: Failed to load NIS2 gap assessment.
nis2Gap.rerunStarted: New re-assessment started. Answer it from the onboarding NIS2 step, then return here — this run joins the history below.
nis2Gap.applyFailed: Failed to create the approved items.
nis2Gap.description: Track your NIS2 maturity over time, see what changed, and turn priority gaps into remediation — you approve before anything is created.
nis2Gap.disclaimer: This is a self-assessment maturity aid, not a legal compliance determination of NIS2 conformance. It informs remediation; it does not certify anything or replace an auditor.
nis2Gap.radarEmpty: No answers yet. Answer the assessment from the NIS2 step (or dispatch it to your team below) — the maturity radar populates as answers come in.
nis2Gap.approveHint: Approve the remediations to create — nothing is written until you confirm.
nis2Gap.confirmDesc: This creates {count} item(s) — risks, controls, and tasks — from the gaps you approved. Existing NIS2 controls are re-used (a task is bound to them) rather than duplicated. This is reversible.
nis2Gap.assignHint: Route each part of the assessment to the person who can answer it. Each member sees only their questions.

respond.submitted: Your answers were submitted. Thank you — the assessment owner will finalise the run.
respond.title: Your NIS2 questions
respond.description: Answer only the questions assigned to you. Your answers feed the shared assessment; the owner finalises it.
respond.alreadySubmitted: You have already submitted this assignment. You can update your answers and re-submit.

findings.title: Findings Register
findings.listDescription: Issues raised in audit and assessment — owned, dated, closed.
findings.selectFinding: Select a finding to view details

list.emptyDesc: Findings capture nonconformities, observations, and opportunities — what an audit surfaced and what needs follow-up.

create.title: New finding
create.headerDesc: Capture a nonconformity, observation, or opportunity — with its owner, controls, and implicated risks.
create.placeholderTitle: e.g. Access reviews not performed quarterly
create.descLinkedControl: The control this finding is raised against.
create.descCompensatingControl: A control that mitigates the finding.

mapping.title: Framework Mapping
mapping.subtitle: Cross-framework readiness view. All descriptions are original paraphrases.

soaView.unmappedReqs: {count, plural, one {# unmapped requirement} other {# unmapped requirements}}
soaView.missingJust: {count, plural, one {# missing justification} other {# missing justifications}}
soaView.noGaps: No gaps found — all requirements are mapped with justifications!
soaView.mapModalDesc: Map a tenant control to requirement {code}.
soaView.mapModalHeaderDesc: Select a tenant control to map to this Annex A requirement.
soaView.justHeaderRich: Justify why control {controlCode} is not applicable for requirement {requirementCode}.
soaView.justModalDesc: Justify why {controlCode} is not applicable for {requirementCode}.
soaView.justPlaceholder: e.g. Fully remote company — no physical premises to secure.

soaPrint.printHint: Use Ctrl+P (or Cmd+P) to print or save as PDF
soaPrint.footer: Generated by Inflect Compliance on {date}. This document is a point-in-time snapshot and should be verified against current controls.

admin.title: Administration
admin.templateDescription: Available policy templates to quick-start your compliance documentation.

sso.title: SSO & Identity
sso.subtitle: Configure enterprise single sign-on for your workspace.
sso.nameIdTooltip: Leave empty if your IdP issues email-format NameIDs (the common case). Override only if your IdP uses a non-standard URN — check the IdP's SAML attribute mapping.
sso.enforceTooltip: Everyone must sign in via your IdP once this saves. Local-password admins keep break-glass access — confirm at least one admin has a working password before enabling.
sso.enforcedNotice: When enforced, users must authenticate via SSO. Only admins with a local password can bypass (break-glass access).
sso.how1: Users must already have an account and tenant membership to login via SSO
sso.how2: SSO links are created automatically on first successful login
sso.how3: When enforced, only admins with a local password can bypass SSO (break-glass)
sso.how4: Email domains help auto-discover SSO on the login page
sso.deleteDesc: Users will need to use local login until SSO is reconfigured. This cannot be undone.

scim.title: SCIM Provisioning
scim.subtitle: Automate user provisioning from your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin).
scim.endpointHint: Use this base URL when configuring SCIM in your identity provider.
scim.newTokenBody: This token will not be shown again. Store it securely in your identity provider.
scim.copyToastOk: SCIM token copied — paste it into your IdP now.
scim.copyToastFail: Copy failed — select the token and copy manually.
scim.errNoPermission: You do not have permission to view SCIM tokens.
scim.revokedCount: {count, plural, one {# revoked token} other {# revoked tokens}}
scim.setup1: Generate a SCIM token above and copy it securely.
scim.setup2: In your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, etc.), configure a SCIM 2.0 provisioning connector.
scim.setup5: Test the connection from your IdP’s SCIM provisioning settings.
scim.revokeDesc: Any IdP using this token will lose access immediately. This cannot be undone.
scim.setup3: Set the SCIM connector base URL to the endpoint shown above.
scim.setup4: Set Authentication to "HTTP Header" with the bearer token.
scim.setupActions: Enable provisioning actions: Create Users, Update User Attributes, Deactivate Users.
scim.roleMapBody: Role mapping: SCIM-provisioned users are assigned the Reader role by default. Editors and Auditors can be mapped via your IdP\s group/role assignment. Admin role cannot be assigned via SCIM — it must be set manually.
scim.emptyTokensHint: Generate a token to enable automated provisioning from your identity provider.

entra.title: Microsoft Entra ID
entra.saveFailedGuids: Save failed — check the directory + client IDs are valid GUIDs.
entra.enforceGateHint: When on, a user who matches none of your group → role mappings is denied access. Only takes effect once you’ve added at least one mapping below.

mappings.title: 3 · Group → role mappings
mappings.description: Map an Entra security group to an IC role. When a user signs in, the highest-priority matching group sets their role. Ownership is never assigned by group — it stays manually granted.
mappings.errExists: A mapping for this group already exists.
mappings.errDenied: Access denied — only admins can manage mappings.
mappings.errInvalidGuid: Add failed — the group ID must be a valid GUID.
mappings.errRemove: Failed to remove the mapping — it has been restored.
mappings.emptyDesc: Add a mapping below to drive roles from Entra group membership.
mappings.roleHint: OWNER is granted manually, not by group.

roles.title: Custom Roles
roles.headerDesc: {count, plural, one {# custom role} other {# custom roles}} defined. Members assigned a custom role use its permissions instead of the built-in role defaults.
roles.emptyState: No custom roles defined yet. Click "Create Custom Role" to get started.
roles.baseRoleHint: Used for coarse authorization when custom permissions don't apply.
roles.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — describe what this role is for
roles.deleteMemberWarning: {count, plural, one {# member} other {# members}} will lose this custom role and fall back to their base role.
roles.deletedSuccess: Deleted "{name}". {count, plural, one {# member} other {# members}} reassigned to fallback.

members.title: Members & Roles
members.sessionsDesc: Live sessions for this member. Revoke any device to sign it out on its next request.
members.activeSessionsCount: {count, plural, one {# active session.} other {# active sessions.}}
members.sessionsEmptyBody: This user is not currently signed in on any device.
members.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate {email}? They will lose access to this tenant.
members.revokeSessionConfirm: Revoke this session? The user will be signed out from this device on their next request.

security.title: Security & MFA
security.mfaPolicyTooltip: Applies tenant-wide on the next login. Switching to Required forces everyone who isn't enrolled into the enrolment flow before they can access any page.
security.mfaPolicyDesc: Configure whether MFA is required, optional, or disabled for your organization.

DISABLED.label: Disabled
DISABLED.description: MFA is not available. Users cannot enroll in multi-factor authentication.

OPTIONAL.label: Optional
OPTIONAL.description: Users can choose to enable MFA. Enrolled users will be challenged at login.

REQUIRED.label: Required
REQUIRED.description: All users must enroll in MFA. Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on login.

security.beforeRequired1: Ensure you (the admin) have enrolled in MFA first
security.beforeRequired2: Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on their next login
security.beforeRequired3: Break-glass admin access is preserved via SSO if configured
security.sessionSettingsDesc: Configure session timeout for your organization. Leave blank for the default.
security.maxSessionAgeTooltip: Absolute lifetime of a login session — users must re-authenticate after this many minutes regardless of activity. Leave blank to inherit the platform default.
security.sessionMgmtDesc: Revoke active sessions. Revoked users must sign in again.
security.signOutOthersDesc: Invalidate all your active sessions across devices.
security.revokeAllDesc: Force all organization members to sign in again. Use for incidents.
security.revokeSpecificLabel: Revoke sessions for a specific user
security.revokeMyConfirm: This will sign you out of all devices. Continue?
security.mySessionsRevoked: Your sessions have been revoked. You will be signed out shortly.
security.revokeAllConfirm: WARNING: This will sign out ALL users in your organization. Are you sure?
security.allRevoked: Sessions revoked for {count} users. Everyone will need to sign in again.

mcp.title: MCP
mcp.description: Agent (Model Context Protocol) human-in-the-loop tools — the approval queue and the orchestrator.
mcp.proposalsDesc: Review the propose-not-commit queue. Approve or reject the writes external agents submit via the MCP propose tools — nothing is created until a human approves.
mcp.runsDesc: Observe the orchestrator. Start a workflow, watch its step timeline and cost, resume a paused run after acting on its proposals, or abort a runaway run.
mcp.receiptsDesc: Externally-verifiable, mediator-signed (pipelock) evidence of agent tool actions. Each verified Ed25519 receipt links to the hash-chained audit trail; export any receipt for independent verification.
mcp.receiptsPageDesc: Externally-verifiable, mediator-signed evidence of agent tool actions (pipelock). Verified receipts link to the hash-chained audit trail.
mcp.receiptsEmpty: No agent-action receipts yet. Once the pipelock mediator is in front of the MCP surface, each mediated tool decision posts a signed receipt here.
mcp.signatureVerifiedTooltip: Ed25519 signature verified; linked to the hash-chained audit trail.
mcp.unverifiedTooltip: Signature invalid or absent — retained + flagged, not linked to the audit trail.

sharepoint.title: Microsoft SharePoint
sharepoint.description: Source compliance evidence from SharePoint document libraries and keep policies in sync. Connecting grants IC delegated read access to the sites you choose.
sharepoint.consentError: Could not start the SharePoint consent flow.
sharepoint.chooseSites: Choose which sites IC may access:

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.emptyConfigured: No integrations configured. Click "Add Integration" to get started.
integrations.secretsNotice: Secrets are encrypted at rest. They cannot be viewed after saving.

forbidden.title: Admin Access Required
forbidden.message: You do not have permission to access admin settings. Contact your workspace administrator to request access.

billing.title: Billing
billing.description: Manage your workspace plan and billing for {name}.
billing.trialDaysLeft: {count, plural, one {# day left in your trial} other {# days left in your trial}}
billing.trialEndsOn: Trial ends on {date}. Upgrade to keep access to premium features.
billing.paymentIssueBody: Please update your payment method to avoid service interruption.
billing.selfHostedBody: This instance runs without a Stripe integration — plan limits resolve to ENTERPRISE for every tenant and the in-app upgrade / portal buttons are disabled. Subscription changes are managed by your operator.
billing.manageSubscriptionBody: Update payment method, view invoices, or change your plan via the Stripe Customer Portal.

apiKeys.copyWarning: Copy this key now — it will never be shown again!
apiKeys.fullAccessTooltip: Gives this key read + write on every resource — evidence, controls, admin settings. Prefer narrow scopes for automation.
apiKeys.copyToast: API key copied — paste it into your tool now.
apiKeys.copyFailedToast: Copy failed — select the key and copy manually.
apiKeys.createdSuccess: API key "{name}" created. Copy the key below.
apiKeys.pageDescription: Manage machine-to-machine API keys for programmatic access. Keys are scoped to specific resources and actions.
apiKeys.expiryTooltip: Keys with no expiry stay valid until someone manually revokes them. Set a deadline for any automation or third-party integration.
apiKeys.revokeConfirmDesc: Key prefix {prefix}… will stop working immediately. Any integration using it will lose access. This cannot be undone.

notifications.runJobConfirm: Are you sure you want to run the {jobType} job now?
notifications.title: Email Notifications
notifications.bccNote: All outbound emails will be BCC'd to this address.

riskAppetite.title: Risk Appetite
riskAppetite.percentileHint: simulated portfolio percentile (falls back to Σ of means until a simulation runs)

riskMatrix.title: Risk matrix configuration
riskMatrix.description: Tenant-scoped likelihood × impact dimensions, axis labels, and severity bands. Changes apply to every risk page in this tenant on save.
riskMatrix.bandsCoverHint: Bands must cover scores 1..{count} without gaps or overlaps.
riskMatrix.previewHint: Synthetic 1-risk-per-cell payload — colour bands + axis labels reflect the current draft.
riskMatrix.toastNoRange: No score range left for another band.

trustCenter.title: Trust Center
trustCenter.intro: Compose a public, curated compliance page. It is OFF until an OWNER publishes it.
trustCenter.postureSummaryDesc: Prose you write — never a live count.
trustCenter.frameworksDesc: You set the status label — it is not derived from coverage.
trustCenter.publishedDocumentsDesc: Links to already-public artifacts only — never internal evidence.
trustCenter.allowIndexing: Allow search engines to index this page
trustCenter.confirmTitle: Publish this page to the public internet?
trustCenter.confirmDesc: This page will be publicly accessible at {path} with no login required. Confirm the content above contains only information safe to publish.

vendorTemplates.title: Vendor questionnaire templates
vendorTemplates.description: Author and publish reusable assessment templates. Edits on a published template require cloning to a new draft revision.
vendorTemplates.emptyDesc: Use the form above to create your first vendor questionnaire template.

templateBuilder.publishedDesc: Edits to a published template would invalidate any live assessments pinned to its version. Clone it to a new draft revision to continue editing.
templateBuilder.promptPlaceholder: Do you encrypt data at rest?
templateBuilder.questionsEmpty: No questions yet — drag here or add one below.

assessmentReview.awaitingDesc: The vendor hasn't submitted yet. Review controls unlock once the assessment moves to SUBMITTED.

riskManager.title: Risks
riskManager.startScratchDesc: Create a custom risk with your own title, description, and scoring.
riskManager.useTemplateDesc: Start from a pre-built risk template with recommended scoring.
riskManager.noTemplates: No templates available. Try creating from scratch.
riskManager.noPermission: You don't have permission to create risks.
riskManager.csvDesc: Upload a CSV file with at minimum a title column. Optional columns: description, category, likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), owner.
riskManager.noValidRows: No valid rows found. Ensure CSV has a "title" column header.
riskManager.noImportPermission: You don't have permission to import risks.
riskManager.noControlsAvailable: No controls available. Add controls first from the Controls page.

board.title: Risk Board
board.positionBody: The portfolio loss the program plans reserves for — the year you'd see in roughly 1 of every 5.
board.positionEmpty: Not quantified yet — run a portfolio simulation on the operational dashboard to populate the board exposure figure. No fabricated number.
board.appetiteEmpty: Set a portfolio loss ceiling on the appetite configuration page to anchor the board's tolerance.
board.topEmpty: No quantified risks yet — once a risk carries SLE × ARO or FAIR inputs, the contributors list ranks itself.
board.bestValueEmpty: No control yet carries a price + an effectiveness signal + a quantified linked risk. Price the highest-leverage controls first.
board.hygieneBody: {stale} of {total} risks carry a stale assessment ({pct}%) — overdue review, untouched past the age ceiling, or a signal that has moved since.

dash.quantEmpty: No risks quantified yet — open a risk and set SLE × ARO on the overview, or fill the FAIR inputs on the Quantification tab, to populate the loss-exceedance curve and top-by-ALE table here.
dash.sumLine: Σ of mean ALEs: {amount} — a sum of averages, not a distribution.
dash.sumTooltip: Summing each risk's mean ALE ignores correlation and tail compounding — bad years cluster. The simulated percentiles above are the portfolio's actual loss distribution: P50 is a typical year, P80/P95 are the years the board plans reserves for. The gap between the sum and these figures is real information, not noise.
dash.sumNudge: These figures are sums of averages. Run a simulation below to replace them with calibrated portfolio percentiles.
dash.coherenceAgree: Qualitative scores and loss estimates agree across the {count} quantified risks — no rank contradictions detected.
dash.coherenceDisagree: {count, plural, one {# risk} other {# risks}} where the matrix and the money disagree — one of the two assessments is probably wrong.
dash.coherenceBigger: score {score} vs {ale} — losses say this is bigger
dash.coherenceSmaller: score {score} vs {ale} — losses say this is smaller
dash.staleBody: {stale} of {total} risks carry an assessment the world may have moved past — overdue review, untouched for {days}+ days, or control tests newer than the residual.

vendors.title: Vendor Register
vendors.subtitle: Third-party relationships, assessed and renewed on a cadence.

empty.filtersHint: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
empty.recordsHint: Register sub-processors and suppliers to track DPAs, contracts, and risk reviews in one place.

modal.title: New vendor
modal.description: Create a vendor to track third-party risk.
modal.discardConfirm: Discard vendor? Any details you entered will be lost.

detail.name: Name
detail.title: Title
detail.linkShareHint: The invitation was emailed to the respondent. Share this link manually if needed — it grants access to the questionnaire.
detail.sendModalDesc: Email a published questionnaire to this vendor's respondent.
detail.linksEmptyDesc: Link this vendor to controls, risks, or evidence to surface them here.
detail.freezeConfirm: Freeze this bundle? Items become immutable.
detail.bundlesEmptyDesc: Generate evidence bundles to share assessment artefacts with this vendor.
detail.assessEmptyDesc: Start a new questionnaire to assess this vendor.
detail.subsEmptyDesc: Add a subprocessor to track downstream data-processing relationships.

monitoring.title: Posture monitor
monitoring.timelineEmpty: No posture events yet. Run the monitor to check breaches, attestation expiry, and TLS grade.

dashboard.title: Vendor Dashboard
dashboard.expiringDocsHint: Review vendor documents tab to check validity dates.

assessment.submitConfirm: Submit this assessment for review? Answers will be locked.
assessment.weightTooltip: Question weight — multiplies the response score in the overall risk calculation.

prefill.heading: Pre-fill from a document
prefill.description: AI reads a SOC 2 / ISO / pen-test report and proposes cited answers. Nothing is scored until you approve.
prefill.proposalsFailedMsg: No proposed answers — the AI extraction did not succeed.
prefill.proposalsFromDocMsg: No proposed answers from this document.

list.heading: Compliance Frameworks
list.description: Browse standards, install control packs, and track requirement coverage
list.empty: No frameworks available. Run the seed to populate.
list.customDescription: Where customisation lives today, and what's coming.
list.customToday: Today — the canonical path is to import a catalogue framework (ISO27001, NIS2, …) and then customise per requirement on the framework's Customize tab. Reorder requirements, link controls, and add per-tenant metadata — every change is tenant-scoped.
list.customComingSoon: Coming soon — tenant-scoped frameworks built from scratch. The current Framework model is global (one Framework row shared across every tenant); fully bespoke frameworks need a tenantId column + matching RLS policies. Queued behind the larger framework-lifecycle epic so the migration lands with the rest of the customisation surface.

detail.reorderRestricted: Reordering is restricted to OWNER / ADMIN roles.
detail.packsEmpty: No packs available for this framework.

diff.heading: Requirements Diff
diff.specifyFrom: Specify a ?from=FRAMEWORK_KEY query parameter to compare.
diff.specifyHint: This page compares the "from" framework to this framework to show added/removed/changed requirements.
diff.computeFailed: Failed to compute diff. Ensure both frameworks exist.

install.heading: Install {name} Pack
install.description: This will create controls, tasks, and requirement mappings for your tenant.
install.packsEmpty: No packs available for this framework.

readiness.created: Created {created} findings and {tasks} tasks · reopened {reopened} · closed {closed}.
readiness.heading: NIS2 readiness
readiness.disclaimer: This is a self-assessment maturity aid, not a legal compliance determination of NIS2 conformance. It informs remediation; it does not certify anything or replace an auditor.
readiness.focusHint: Focus your control baseline on the lowest-scoring domains: {domains}.
readiness.confirmTitle: Create findings + tasks from these gaps?
readiness.confirmDesc: This will create up to {count} findings (HIGH/CRITICAL gaps) and a remediation task for each. Re-running reconciles: gaps you've since resolved are closed. This is reversible — you can close or delete the findings.
readiness.onlyNis2: Self-assessment readiness is only available for the NIS2 framework.

selfAssessment.heading: NIS2 self-assessment
selfAssessment.resumeHint: Pick up where you left off. Answers save automatically.
selfAssessment.onlyNis2: The gap self-assessment is only available for the NIS2 framework.

list.description: Compliance work prioritised, assigned, and tracked to closure.
list.emptyDesc: Tasks track remediation work — anything that turns a finding or open risk into a closed loop.
list.filterEmptyDesc: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.

new.modalDesc: Create a new task to track.
new.discardConfirm: Discard task? Any details you entered will be lost.
new.title: Title

detail.chooseFileOrUrl: Choose a file to upload, or enter an evidence URL.
detail.evidenceLoadErrorDesc: Something went wrong fetching this task's evidence. Reload the page to try again.
detail.commentsEmptyDesc: Use the comment box above to leave context, observations, or questions.
detail.activityEmptyDesc: Status changes, assignments, and link updates show up here once anything moves.
detail.terminalDesc: Add a short resolution note for the audit trail.
detail.terminalHeaderDesc: A resolution note is recorded on the audit trail.
detail.resolutionPlaceholder: What was done / why it's being closed
detail.linksEmptyDesc: Cross-link this task to related tasks, controls, evidence, or risks via + Link.

dashboard.title: Task Dashboard
dashboard.myTasksEmpty: No open tasks assigned to you
dashboard.topLinked: Top Assets & Risks with Open Tasks

processes.mobileHint: Process maps are edited on a larger screen. Open this page on a desktop to design the canvas.

rules.title: Rules
rules.emptyDesc: Automation rules fire actions when domain events occur. The rule builder arrives in a later release.

analytics.emptyDesc: Create a rule to start seeing execution analytics here.

governance.heading: Governance graph
governance.description: Every automation map, sized by rule volume and ringed by 30-day execution health, with the sub-flow calls between them.
governance.empty: No process maps yet — create an automation workflow to populate the graph.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.description: NIS2 Article 23 incident response. Reporting thresholds + deadlines are operational aids — they are not legal advice; your DPO/legal owns the determination.
incidents.emptyDesc: When a security incident is detected, open it here to start the NIS2 Article 23 response clock.

new.title: Open incident
new.desc: Record a live security incident to start the NIS2 Article 23 response.
new.descLong: Record a live security incident to start the NIS2 Article 23 response clock.

placeholders.title: e.g. Ransomware on the billing cluster
placeholders.description: What happened, what's affected, what's known so far

new.detectedHint: The clock that drives the 24h / 72h / 1-month deadlines.

detail.notLegalBody: The NIS2 Article 23 reportability threshold and the 24h / 72h / 1-month deadlines below are operational aids. Your DPO/legal team owns the actual reporting determination.
detail.runbookDesc: First-response containment steps for a {type} incident. Operational guidance, not legal advice. Completing a step records it on the timeline.
detail.notReportableText: This incident is not yet marked reportable. Marking it reportable is a human determination (not automatic, not legal advice) that starts the 24h / 72h / 1-month clocks.
detail.authorityText: The competent CSIRT / authority you notify is jurisdiction-specific (e.g. CCB in Belgium, NCSC-NL in the Netherlands, BSI in Germany). Configure it under <settings>tenant security settings</settings>.
detail.forensicDesc: Capture forensic evidence for each category and link the real Evidence records so the incident stays audit-ready.
detail.controlsEmpty: No controls linked yet. Link the Art.21(2) controls this incident implicates from the control detail page or via the API.
detail.fileDesc: Record the report you filed with the competent authority.
detail.reportTextPlaceholder: The report submitted to the authority
detail.caseRefHint: The reference the authority returned, if any.
detail.addTimelineDesc: Record an action or observation on this incident.
detail.markReportableDesc: This is a human determination — not automatic and not legal advice.
detail.markReportableBody: Marking this incident reportable under NIS2 Article 23 starts the 24-hour early-warning, 72-hour detailed-report, and 1-month final-report deadline clocks (derived from the detection time). Confirm only if your DPO/legal has determined the incident meets the significant-impact threshold.
detail.linkForensicDesc: Attach an existing tenant Evidence record to this incident.
detail.forensicCategoryHint: Which checklist category this evidence satisfies.

list.title: Tests
list.description: Test plans and recent results across all controls
list.emptyFiltered: No test plans match your filters.
list.emptyNone: No test plans found. Create test plans from the Control detail page.

due.title: Due Queue
due.description: Test plans due or overdue for execution
due.planningResult: Due planning complete: checked {checked} plans, created {created} new runs, {alreadyPending} already had pending runs.
due.empty: No tests are due! All plans are on schedule.

dashboard.title: Test Dashboard
dashboard.noCompleted: No completed runs in this period
dashboard.fwCoverageDesc: How well your mapped controls are covered by active test plans and recent test runs

errors.linkFailed: Failed to link evidence to the run

run.title: Test Run
run.retestHint: Create a new run for this test plan

proposals.accessMessage: You do not have permission to review agent proposals. Contact your workspace administrator to request access.
proposals.title: Agent proposals
proposals.description: Pending proposals from an external agent (MCP). Approve to create the real record; reject to discard. An agent never creates a record directly.
proposals.emptyDesc: When an agent proposes a risk, control, policy, or finding via the MCP server, it appears here for your review.

runs.accessMessage: You do not have permission to view agent runs. Contact your workspace administrator to request access.
runs.title: Agent runs
runs.description: Multi-step agentic workflows. Every write a run makes is a proposal a human approves — the engine commits nothing directly.
runs.emptyDesc: Start a workflow above. Each run reads your compliance data, proposes changes for your approval, and produces a summary.
runs.awaitingApprovalPre: Paused for review — approve its proposals in the

securityTesting.title: Security Testing
securityTesting.descEmpty: Scanner findings ingested from your CI via SARIF.
securityTesting.descActive: {runs, plural, one {# recent run} other {# recent runs}} · {critical, plural, one {# open critical finding} other {# open critical findings}}.
securityTesting.emptyDesc: POST a SARIF report from your CI to /api/t/{slug}/security-testing/ingest. A passing scan attaches automated evidence to its mapped control; failing findings appear here and in the Findings register.

calendar.title: Compliance Calendar
calendar.description: Track every compliance deadline — evidence reviews, policy renewals, vendor renewals, audit cycles, control tests — in one place.
calendar.loadError: Failed to load calendar events. Try refreshing.
calendar.ganttEmpty: No duration-based events in this range. Create an audit cycle to see it here.
calendar.clickDay: Click a day to see events on that date.

coverage.title: Coverage Dashboard
coverage.description: How your assets are protected by controls and what risks remain unmitigated
coverage.emptyAssets: All critical assets are covered by controls
coverage.emptyRisks: All risks are mitigated by controls

accessReviews.title: Access Reviews
accessReviews.emptyList: No access reviews yet. Click New campaign to start one.
accessReviews.descPlaceholder: Focus and rationale for this campaign
accessReviews.closeBody: Closing executes every REVOKE and MODIFY decision against the live tenant memberships. CONFIRM decisions stay untouched. A signed PDF evidence artifact will be generated and linked to the campaign. This cannot be undone.

frameworkUpdates.title: Framework updates
frameworkUpdates.description: When a framework version lands, here's exactly what changed and what it means for you — new gaps and controls to re-review.
frameworkUpdates.emptyDescription: When a framework you have installed publishes a new version, its personalised delta-gap appears here.
frameworkUpdates.materialised: Materialised {count} finding(s) for the new gaps.

vulnerabilities.title: Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: CVEs matched to your assets — convert critical ones into risks or findings.
vulnerabilities.emptyDesc: CVEs appear here once the NVD sync matches a published CVE to an asset with CPE/product data, or when you link one manually.

mfa.tooManyAttempts: Too many failed attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
mfa.invalidCode: Invalid code. Please check your authenticator app and try again.
mfa.enrollRequiredBody: Your organization requires multi-factor authentication. Please set up MFA to continue.
mfa.verifyBody: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to continue.
mfa.cantAccess: Can't access your authenticator? Contact your organization administrator.
mfa.enrolledSuccess: MFA enrolled successfully! Your account is now protected.
mfa.keyCopied: Setup key copied — paste it into your authenticator app.
mfa.copyFailed: Copy failed — select the key and copy manually.
mfa.orgRequires: Your organization requires MFA. You must enroll to continue using the application.
mfa.step1: 1. Open your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.)
mfa.step2: 2. Scan this QR code or manually enter the setup key
mfa.cannotScan: If you cannot scan, use the setup key below
mfa.step3: 3. Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app
mfa.removeConfirmDesc: Your account will be less secure. You can re-enroll at any time.
